full_name: Ministry of Post-Secondary Education and Future Skills
blurb: Oversees universities, colleges and skills training — StudentAid BC, apprenticeship through SkilledTradesBC, and workforce readiness.

goals.title: GOAL 1
goals.short_name: Post-secondary education in B.C. is accessible and affordable
goals.description: The ministry oversees B.C.'s public post-secondary system and StudentAid BC so students can find the right program, pay for their studies, and complete adult upgrading, English language learning and Indigenous-led education.

main_services.name: Choose and apply to a post-secondary program

supporting.name: Find a post-secondary institution or program
supporting.desc: Search public, private, theological and Aboriginal-controlled post-secondary institutions authorized to offer education and training in B.C., and use Find Your Path to match programs to career goals.

legislation.full_name: College and Institute Act - Continuation and operation of colleges and institutes
University Act - Continuation and operation of universities

supporting.name: Apply to a public post-secondary program through EducationPlannerBC
supporting.desc: Build a personal education plan and apply online to any of B.C.'s 25 public post-secondary institutions through the EducationPlannerBC portal.

supporting.name: Transfer course credit between B.C. institutions
supporting.desc: Use the BC Transfer Guide to see how course credits transfer between B.C. post-secondary institutions and count toward a credential at another school.

supporting.name: Get credit for prior learning and work experience
supporting.desc: Public post-secondary institutions assess whether skills and knowledge gained through life and work experience are equivalent to a course, so students can obtain prior learning assessment (PLA) credit.

supporting.name: Apply for a student loan
supporting.desc: Apply for an interest-free, integrated Canada-B.C. student loan through StudentAid BC to help cover tuition, fees and living costs while you study, with a single point of contact for repayment.

supporting.name: Apply for grants, scholarships and bursaries
supporting.desc: Explore non-repayable provincial and federal grants, the B.C. Access Grant, disability funding, dependant grants and scholarship programs available through StudentAid BC.

channels.label: Grants, scholarships, and other financial supports

supporting.name: Get an Adult Upgrading Grant for ABE, ESL or adult special education
supporting.desc: Eligible low-income students taking Adult Basic Education, English as a Second Language or Adult Special Education programs at public post-secondary institutions can get a non-repayable grant covering tuition, fees, books and unsubsidized child care.

supporting.name: Get a tuition waiver as a current or former youth in care
supporting.desc: The Provincial Tuition Waiver Program waives tuition and eligible fees for current and former children or youth in care studying at a B.C. public post-secondary institution, the Native Education College, or an approved union-based trades training provider.
supporting.users: Current and former children and youth in government care

relationships.service: Get support transitioning out of foster care as a young adult

main_services.name: Complete adult upgrading and English language learning

supporting.name: Take tuition-free adult upgrading courses to prepare for post-secondary studies
supporting.desc: Adult Basic Education and academic upgrading courses are tuition-free for domestic students at 18 public post-secondary institutions and through school districts, helping adults improve literacy, complete high school credits, or meet post-secondary entrance requirements. The ministry also funds the Community Adult Literacy Program (CALP) in over 200 communities.

channels.label: Public post-secondary institution or school district

supporting.name: Improve your English through adult English language learning programs
supporting.desc: Tuition-free English Language Learning programs at public post-secondary institutions and school districts help adult learners, including newcomers, build the English skills needed for further education, work or daily life.

main_services.name: Access Indigenous post-secondary education and skills training

supporting.name: Access Indigenous post-secondary education programs and funding
supporting.desc: The Indigenous Pathways and Partnerships Program and Indigenous Skills Training and Education (ISTE) program fund culturally relevant post-secondary access, retention and skills training initiatives developed with First Nations, Métis and Indigenous partners.

channels.label: PSFS Indigenous Policy and Engagement Branch

supporting.name: Attend a First Nations mandated post-secondary institute
supporting.desc: First Nations Mandated Post-Secondary Institutes (FNMPSIs) offer programs rooted in Indigenous language and culture; the province provides them ongoing operational and capacity funding as a key pillar of B.C.'s post-secondary system.

channels.label: First Nations Mandated Post-Secondary Institutes Act

legislation.full_name: First Nations Mandated Post-Secondary Institutes Act - Recognition and funding of FNMPSIs

goals.title: GOAL 2
goals.short_name: Skilled trades training builds B.C.'s workforce
goals.description: SkilledTradesBC registers and certifies apprentices, enforces Skilled Trades Certification in compulsory trades, and the ministry funds micro-credentials and labour market information so workers can build in-demand skills.

supporting.name: Register as an apprentice
supporting.desc: Find a sponsor and register your apprenticeship through the SkilledTradesBC Portal to begin combined on-the-job training and in-class technical training toward a Certificate of Apprenticeship.
supporting.users: New and partially experienced trades workers

legislation.full_name: Skilled Trades BC Act - Apprenticeship registration and trades training system

supporting.name: Get certified as a journeyperson through a certification exam
supporting.desc: After completing work-based and in-class technical training, pass a certification exam to obtain a Certificate of Qualification or a nationally recognized Red Seal Endorsement issued by SkilledTradesBC.

legislation.full_name: Skilled Trades BC Act - Certification of journeypersons

supporting.name: Get financial support for your apprenticeship
supporting.desc: Apprentices may access grants, Canada Apprentice Loans, Employment Insurance during technical training, and tax credits, along with child care, travel and lost-wage supports while training.

supporting.name: Meet Skilled Trades Certification requirements in a compulsory trade
supporting.desc: Workers in compulsory (Skilled Trades Certification) trades — including construction and industrial electrician, powerline technician, steamfitter/pipefitter, refrigeration mechanic, gasfitter, sheet metal worker and crane operators — must be a registered apprentice, Trade Qualifier or certified journeyperson.

legislation.full_name: Skilled Trades BC Act - Skilled Trades Certification (compulsory trades) requirements

relationships.service: Sponsor an apprentice as an employer

supporting.name: Sponsor an apprentice as an employer
supporting.desc: Employers sponsoring apprentices must maintain a maximum 2:1 apprentice-to-journeyperson supervision ratio, report work-based training hours, and complete an Employer Workforce Submission for workers in Skilled Trades Certification trades.
supporting.users: Employers and sponsors of trades workers

legislation.full_name: Skilled Trades BC Act - Employer obligations and supervision ratios

relationships.service: Meet Skilled Trades Certification requirements in a compulsory trade
Get hired through the Community Benefits Agreement on a public project

supporting.name: Take a micro-credential program for in-demand skills
supporting.desc: Micro-credentials are short, competency-based programs recognized by employers and post-secondary institutions that help learners quickly gain skills for high-demand jobs, with funding of up to $3,500 available through the StrongerBC future skills grant.
supporting.users: Workers, career-changers and students seeking short-duration training

main_services.name: Find work and labour market information

supporting.name: Search for jobs and labour market information through WorkBC
supporting.desc: Browse job postings, explore careers and high-opportunity occupations, and access training and employment services through WorkBC and its network of WorkBC Centres.

relationships.service: Get help finding a job at an employment services centre

goals.title: GOAL 3
goals.short_name: Post-secondary institutions and training providers meet quality standards
goals.description: The ministry sets Education Quality Assurance standards, authorizes degree-granting institutions, and certifies and enforces standards for private training institutions to protect students and maintain B.C.'s reputation for quality education.

supporting.name: Check if an institution has Education Quality Assurance (EQA) designation
supporting.desc: The EQA designation confirms an institution meets quality assurance standards above legislated requirements; all post-secondary institutions and language schools hosting international students on study permits must hold it.

supporting.name: Check if a private training institution is certified
supporting.desc: The Private Training Institution Directory lists institutions certified by the Private Training Institutions Regulatory Unit (PTIRU) and shows enforcement actions such as suspensions and cancellations.

legislation.full_name: Private Training Act - s.17: Online directory of institutions

relationships.service: Apply for certification to operate a private training institution

supporting.name: Check if an institution is authorized to grant degrees
supporting.desc: Confirm whether a private or out-of-province public institution is authorized under the Degree Authorization Act to grant degrees or use the word 'university' in B.C.

legislation.full_name: Degree Authorization Act - s.3: Granting of degrees and use of "university" restricted

relationships.service: Apply for degree-granting authority in B.C.

main_services.name: Get help with a private training institution

supporting.name: File a complaint or tuition refund claim against a private training institution
supporting.desc: Students can file a complaint with the Private Training Institutions Regulatory Unit or claim against the Student Tuition Protection Fund if a certified institution closes or breaches its obligations.
supporting.users: Students of certified private training institutions

channels.label: General Complaints and Tuition Refund Claims

legislation.full_name: Private Training Act - Part 3: Consumer protection and Student Tuition Protection Fund

relationships.service: Apply for certification to operate a private training institution

main_services.name: Operate a post-secondary institution or training program

supporting.name: Apply for certification to operate a private training institution
supporting.desc: Institutions offering career-related programs of 40+ instructional hours must obtain a certificate from the Private Training Institutions Regulatory Unit, including designation certification if hosting international students, and pay into the Student Tuition Protection Fund.

legislation.full_name: Private Training Act - s.2-10: Certificate required to provide career training programs

relationships.service: Check if a private training institution is certified
File a complaint or tuition refund claim against a private training institution

supporting.name: Apply for degree-granting authority in B.C.
supporting.desc: Private and out-of-province public institutions seeking to grant degrees or use 'university' in their name apply through the Degree Quality Assessment Board's rigorous assessment process, administered via the PSIPS online system.

legislation.full_name: Degree Authorization Act - Ministerial consent to grant or confer a degree

relationships.service: Check if an institution is authorized to grant degrees

goals.title: GOAL 4
goals.short_name: Internationally trained professionals and newcomers can work and settle in B.C.
goals.description: The Office for International Credential Recognition streamlines credential recognition under the International Credentials Recognition Act, and the ministry leads the BC Provincial Nominee Program and newcomer settlement supports.

supporting.name: Have your international professional credentials recognized by a B.C. regulator
supporting.desc: The Office for International Credential Recognition (OICR) oversees 18 regulatory authorities across 29 professions — including engineers, teachers, social workers, veterinarians and early childhood educators — to make credential recognition fairer, faster and more transparent for internationally trained professionals.

legislation.full_name: International Credentials Recognition Act - Superintendent oversight and regulatory authority obligations

main_services.name: Immigrate to and settle in B.C.

supporting.name: Apply through the BC Provincial Nominee Program
supporting.desc: The BC Provincial Nominee Program (BC PNP) is an accelerated pathway to Canadian permanent residence for skilled workers, entrepreneurs and businesses, with employer-support requirements and an online applicant portal.
supporting.users: Skilled workers, entrepreneurs and employers seeking to nominate workers

channels.label: About the BC Provincial Nominee Program

supporting.name: Access newcomer settlement services and guides
supporting.desc: WelcomeBC provides the B.C. Newcomers' Guide and a resource directory covering healthcare enrolment, housing, driving, banking and settlement services for people moving to B.C.

supporting.name: Access free mental health counselling through Here2Talk
supporting.desc: Here2Talk connects all domestic and international students currently registered at a B.C. post-secondary institution with free, confidential counselling and community referral services, available 24/7 by app, phone and web.

foundational_services.name: Public post-secondary institution funding, accountability and capital planning
foundational_services.desc: Sets annual budget letters, the accountability framework, financial reporting requirements and capital planning guidance for B.C.'s 25 public post-secondary institutions.
foundational_services.users: Internal program staff, public post-secondary institutions

legislation.full_name: College and Institute Act - Governance and funding of colleges and institutes
University Act - Governance and funding of universities

foundational_services.name: Private training and international education compliance monitoring
foundational_services.desc: Inspects private training institutions, monitors compliance with the Education Quality Assurance Code of Practice, and administers enforcement actions including compliance orders, administrative penalties, suspensions and cancellations.
foundational_services.users: Internal program staff, Private Training Institutions Regulatory Unit

legislation.full_name: Private Training Act - Part 4: Inspections, compliance orders and administrative penalties

foundational_services.name: Labour market and skills training data analysis
foundational_services.desc: Analyzes labour market trends, high-opportunity occupations and post-secondary outcomes data to inform funding decisions for micro-credentials, apprenticeship seats and the post-secondary funding formula.
